Will lodge complaint with Marriott directly for the unsatisfying stay, see you

reviews.title: Not the Marriott standard
reviews.text:
wedding takes place every night during our stays.  It felt like we were joining their wedding instead of enjoying our private time. Everywhere you go, you walked into either their guests or the organizers. No privacy whatsoever here outside of your room. Yes that includes pools and beaches because that's where the wedding takes place too!

There are no plates or forks nor knives in the room, not even a coffee plates. When we tried to ask for it (we had some fruits that needed to prepare) we were told that there would be  cost of borrowing for each of the items that were not made available in a guest room.

 We have a bottle of wine and champaign so we called up the operator just to be told that there would be  cost of borrowing for each of the wine glass if we want to drink wine or champaign properly. So we ended up drinking it from a normal glass provided in the room.

The shower holder was broken before we checked in, we were afraid they would think that we did break it so we called to inform them. They apologized, which is nice, and they sent someone to fix it... using glue... At the end, it broke again when we checked out. Hopefully they won't bill us for this.

If these above were Marriott Standards, then I apologize for giving it such terrible review.  My 2 nights vacation would have been more memorable and we were truly disappointed by these.  We could have gone to other resorts in Rayong that is far more serviced minded policies and a whole lot cheaper.

reviews.title: Not a good Experience... :(
reviews.text:
Well, first of all, I could not say, this is a Marriott. I made well negative Experiences and I will not book a Marriott again (or book only the Marriott, I am convinced, that they are a REAL Marriott).
On our arrival Day, we got our Mountain view Room, with a nice view on a Construction, which started the next morning at 07.00 am to work. Of course, it was impossible to get further sleep. Good morning Marriott...
There where no ameninity, like I was used to get at the Marriott or Renaissance (which belongs to Marriott). Totally not acceptable.
The Toilette was leaking in the Bathroom, and the result of that was, a water filled floor in the Bathroom...
There where also NO AIR EXTRACTOR, neither in the Bathroom, nor in the Room itself) This was very annoying and usually, they have in 2-3 star hotels air-extractors, but unbelievably not here in the Marriott Rayong.
And the worst experience is, 3 Huge cockroaches in the Room, when I woke up in the Night (I made Pictures of them on the Spot and they where about 2 inch each).
I also hurt myself badly on the sliding door of the Bathroom, which is really not user-friendly.
After our first and really horrible night, I went to the Reception to ask for another Room.
I was ready for arguing, but there was no necessary, as the Staff where very helpful and we got a "free upgrade" to the sea-view room. Thank you for that Marriott, but it still doesn't reimbuse our first bad night...

The Breakfast is definitely NOT a Marriott like Breakfast. Absolutely not satisfied with the Breakfast, for which i was really looking forward, but unfortunately, I got a big disappointment. Also, We asked 3 Times to get after 10 Minutes our 2 simple coffee's. 
This was very annoying, as the hotel was really not busy at all...

I am very disappointed of the Marriott Chain and will look in future for other Brands...

reviews.title: Marriott Rayong
reviews.text: Bed experiences, Poor staffs performance

reviews.title: Acceptable
reviews.text:
I understand that the hotel has been in service for around 13 years, and I visited during a long holiday period, so I expected prices to be higher than usual. However, I felt that some of the facilities did not quite match the level typically associated with an upper-class or 5-star hotel (as advertised).

During my stay, I noticed a bit of sand on the bed even before using it. The hotel’s private beach is a lovely feature, though it would be even more convenient if simple items like a beach bag were provided for guests to carry their belongings.

On the second day, there was a system issue where my key card did not work. I was unable to access the elevator or enter my room and had to rely on assistance from the concierge and housekeeping staff, which was a bit inconvenient.

From my perspective, some of these experiences may reflect opportunities for improvement at a management or operational level, particularly in maintaining facility standards and ensuring system reliability for guests.

That said, I truly enjoyed the peaceful beach atmosphere and the beautiful sunset views. The staff were very kind and service-minded, which I sincerely appreciate.

Thank you again for your hospitality.
reviews.room_type: Guest room, 1 King, Mountain view

reviews.title: Terrible Customer Service!
reviews.text:
I already told the hotel that I was going to arrive about 16.00-17.00 pm and confirmed in a few days earlier.  When I arrived at the hotel (around 16.05 pm) the staff told me that my room type was unavailable to me because the current guest in the room requested for late check out at 17:00 pm! and told me to wait until 18:00 pm for check in since housekeeper need an additional 1 hr to clean the room. 

Later that night, a storm hit the coast of Rayong. There was water running down the ceiling of a 5-star hotel! Balcony doors can’t handle the wind and slam open at 02:00 am. with the sounds of water running down the leaking ceiling and window edges. Things let even worse when the electricity shuts down. Hotel was running from back up generator until the next morning. So the plugs in the room are not working. We can’t charge our phones and another electric devices. 

The next morning, we called for a request of baggage pick-up. Tried calling frontdesk for about 15 minutes. No answer. 

Worse hotel period.

By the way, my room type was corner suite.

reviews.title: สิ่งที่ได้ ไม่คุ้มราคาที่จ่ายไป ิึ
reviews.text:
ห้องพัก ราคานี้ถ้าไป Amari/Dusit Thani ที่พัทยา หัวหิน จะได้ใช้สิทธิ์ club lounge เลยด้วยซ้ำ แถมโซนนั้นจะมีกิจกรรม เช่น สอนโยคะให้ด้วย 

นอกจากนี้ก็ตกใจมากที่ระดับ Marriott ยังคงให้เป็นเนสกาแฟซอง ในขณะที่ราคานี้จังหวัดท่องเที่ยวอื่นให้เป็นกาแฟแคปซูลอย่างดีกันแล้ว

 อาหารเช้าที่จ่ายเพิ่มเป็นพัน ถือว่ามีตัวเลือกที่น้อยมากจนน่าเสียใจ เครื่องดื่มชา/กาแฟเป็นแบบให้ลูกค้าเดินมาเลือกกดเอง แต่ถ้างงๆก็จะมีพนักงานมาช่วย ในขณะที่ถ้าเป็นโรงแรมอื่นจะมีพนักงานคอยถามตอนพาไปส่งที่โต๊ะ  

โดยรวมแล้วผิดหวัง และคิดว่าโชคดีที่ยังไม่ได้ตัดสินใจสมัคร Marriott’s Ambassador และเลือกที่จะไม่จองราคาผ่านโรงแรมโดยตรง ไม่งั้นคงเจ็บใจมากกว่านี้
เพราะนี่เป็นครั้งแรกที่อยากออกไปเที่ยวข้างนอก ไม่รู้สึกว่าอยากใช้เวลาอยู่ในโรงแรม (ปกติไปพักเครือโรงแรมอื่นจะอยากอยู่/ใช้เวลา/ทำกิจกรรมในโรงแรม ไม่อยากออกไปเที่ยวข้างนอก)

อย่างไรก็ตามพนักงานมีความตั้งใจที่จะดูแลลูกค้า พยายามตอบสนองความต้องการลูกค้าเต็มที่ และ complementary upgrade ห้องให้ 1 ห้อง  ทุกคนดูพยายามจะทำเต็มที่เท่าที่จะทำได้
ดังนั้นความรู้สึกไม่ประทับใจต่างๆที่เกิดขึ้น คิดว่าเป็นความผิดของผู้บริหารผู้วางนโยบายต่างๆให้เป็นแบบนี้มากกว่า ทำให้ลูกค้าเหมือนถูกหลอกให้คาดหวังสูง ด้วยราคาห้อง

นี่นังไม่รวมเรื่องที่ ช่วงนี้สระว่ายน้ำใหญ่ปิดปรับปรุงถึงเดือนกรกฎาคม ก็เข้าใจได้เพราะเป็นหน้าฝน แต่ก็เซ็งหน่อยๆเพราะมารู้หลังจากที่กดจองไปแล้ว จริงๆอย่างน้อยถ้าจะให้แฟร์ก็น่าจะปรับราคาที่พักให้ถูกลง เพื่อเชิญชวนลูกค้าหน้าใหม่เข้ามาสัมผัสประสบการณ์การบริการของ Marriott ซักหน่อย

ปล. ชุด Afternoon tea เค้กไม่ค่อยว้าว เท่าไหร่ (เค้กใบเตยอร่อย) ราคา ~600 บาท ถ้าวจำไม่ผิด แต่ก็ถือว่าถูกกว่าโรงแรมอื่น

reviews.title: There are better options at this price
reviews.text:
The facility looks old and not well maintained. The pools are nice but not well maintained nor cleaned. The restaurant / bar by the pool is a joke: they barely know how to use a microwave or make a decent mojito. We decided to eat outside of the hotel for every meal. 
The staff was nice although service wasn’t timely (we had to call so many times before someone would pick up, food for the dog was not delivered at the agreed upon time (why ask if you wait for us to call everyday?), and the available options to select from for the dog menu were not up to date (F&B 101: check the available dishes before presenting the menu to your guests).  
Few good points though: the breakfast offers variety and quality. And the bed linen was amazing. We slept very well.
All in all… meh. I’ll go to mercure or Novotel next time. It will cost half the price and the service will be at least on par. It’s disappointing from such a big brand.
reviews.room_type: Deluxe King Room with Mountain View

reviews.title: Nice property but could do much better
reviews.text:
I stayed 3 days/2 nights (9 Aug - 11 Aug) at the Marriott in the Ocean View Room on the 9th Floor.
The room... It is very good size with amazing view on the ocean. It was clean with all standard facilities (including connection capabilities for laptop to TV - cables were available on request). All light switches were where you can expect them to be and enough light in the room at night not to feel like a cave. 
While the room is nice, I believe the walls are pretty thin. I could hear all doors closing, people walking next door.
The breakfast... That was probably the worst experience of the (all my hotel) stay. We had to queue (15 min) to get a table at 9.00am. The reception lady just said: "There is a table available over there..." Nobody walked us "there". So we walked in the restaurant and tried to find a free table without finding it. Then we stopped a staff member who looked puzzled when we mentioned that there was an available table... She then walked us to a corner of the room where there were 2 low sofas with low table. We said we can't eat breakfast on this table. It is a pretty bad start of day...
Then we went back to the entrance and waited for a "proper" table. When we asked about the situation, the answer: “this is every weekend”. Which to me does not sound very good...They have the issue every week but did not try to fix to ensure a better customer experience. Most of food stalls were empty. The kitchen could not replenish on time. Ice coffee was more likely like syrup mixed with iced. Eggs were darkened from old oil used, orange juice from supermarket would taste better... 
The pool was far away from the main building, way too crowded. Chairs were full of sand with no one around to clean them. The hotel is (very) far from town. It is not a problem if the plan is to stay in the hotel and eat-in.
It is definitely not a 5 star hotel service. The customer experience could be amazing if the operations were at the same standard of their room quality.

reviews.title: Wonderfully landscaped resort hotel with a view
reviews.text: We went for a relaxing beach holiday but were shocked to find it a hotel catering principally to Indian destination weddings. In fact the wedding party virtually took over the hotel and the high noise and loud music outdoors continued late into the evenings. The F&B outlets including the bar seemed to collapse under the pressure and getting service at the restaurants was a big challenge. The buffet breakfast however was excellent and the only meal we could enjoy. The rooms and the grounds were very nice and our stay would have been perfect had we received service from the F&B outlets! The staff were clearly overworked and too few for the requirement of a 5-star property.

Also, we received wrong information about local tours available. Also many places including pool side was not clean; it was a shame since the infinity pool and its surrounding is quite beautiful.

reviews.title: Poor wi-fi internet connection
reviews.text: I am tired of hotels rating themselves 5-star, charge 5-star prices, promoting free wi-fi in rooms, only for the connection to be very poor.  If you need to do some business work during a stay you often choose a good hotel with wi-fi internet in the rooms.  Unfortunately, despite being able to connect to the internet each time I tried, I was continuously timed out to the point the internet was not usable.  I tried on 5 occasions during my 2 night stay and experienced the same problem.  The excuse provided is not acceptable for a 5-star hotel - "sorry, but when a few people are using the internet at the same time it can have problems".  Simply improve the internet connection, remove your 5-star rating, or dont mislead guests by claiming to provider a service you dont have.

reviews.title: Wrong place to stay
reviews.text: Booked for 8 nights, checked out after 1 night. We arrived at night when a 25th anniversary was being celebrated by about 150(?) Indian guests, including a very,very,very,very loud band that played/sung Indian music. The party went on until deep in the night which we heard on the 7th floor. Distances in the hotel and on the grounds are huge and very inconvenient. The hotel was "swarmed"with people with young children and tourgroups. The location is terrible!! Far from the outside world, so you are a "prisoner"on the resort. Wil never, ever stay here again!! However, having said all this, the quality of the breakfast was excellent, the bed was comfortable and the rainshowe "out of this world". And what has to be said especially is the help of the duty manager who"was bending backwards"to help us. And that world class service is something we will never forget!!

reviews.title: Two night get away
reviews.text:
Pro: the staff go above and beyond. We had a spa and it was out of this world, I recommend it. The pools are nice and clean, the drinks are very yummy. The breakfast is really good. And so is the fish restaurant out by the beach. The bed is very comfy. And the water pressure in the shower is amazing!
Con: the rooms are very dated, some peeling paint. For an American chain (and I get you are in Thailand) all but 2 of the channels on the TV  are in Thai. I would have thought at least an HBO channel for after hours viewing since it is an American chain, but no. I knew this resort was out of town a bit but I didn’t know how much. It is 40min to do any shopping. And even though we drove we thought we could get a Bolt to take us someplace instead of driving and none are available that far out of town. I brought snacks for after hours but had wished I had brought a few more. The nearest 7-11 was at least 5miles away. So not a quick walk. There are a couple of bars on the premises, but all of them get their food from the same restaurant so you get burned out on the same food after a couple of days. 
I would give this 3.5 stars out of 5. We stayed two days and experienced all there was to offer.
reviews.response:
Dear Timothy, 
Thank you for your incredibly detailed and valuable feedback. We're thrilled to hear so many aspects of your stay were exceptional, from our dedicated staff and wonderful spa to the clean pools, delicious drinks, great breakfast, and the amazing fish restaurant. We're also glad you enjoyed the comfy bed and excellent shower pressure!

We sincerely apologize for your disappointment. Your comments are crucial, and we are actively working on mentioned points to better cater to our guests. We truly hope to have the opportunity to welcome you back soon for an even more luxurious experience.

Best Regards,
Rayong Marriott Resort & Spa

reviews.title: Overall was OK
reviews.text:
It is a well equiped hotel and is located in one of the best spot in Rayong coast line. Unfortuantely, as this time is high season in Thailand and so based only upon my experience it was quite disappointed.
1. Though staff was friendly but limited so the check in process taking quite long. We arrived at 14.30 pm but the room wasn't ready so we had to wait until 15:30 to get ours.
2. The room was big and the view from our room was spectacular but there was a little flooding water left in the toilet room which supposed to be dried. Couldn't find slippers in the room but I supposed it will be offered upon request.
3. Swimming pool is really great we enjoyed it a lot.
4. There was a noise from the cieling fan in the room, not too loud but you could always hear while being in the room.
5. In room dining was superb. I recommended Pad Kaprow Moo (Stir fried pork with chilli and basil served with rice) and fried egg and spaghetti with white wine.
6. Breakfast was international but is mainly for westerners while variety of asian food was not too much. Anyway, thai desserts (ทองม้วนสดและ เปียกปูน) are very delicious.
Overall, the kids and my mom really enjoyed the stay and so do I. So I would recommend it anyway.

reviews.title: Lovely hotel but slow service leaves us hungry
reviews.text: We loved the rooms, pools, and buffet breakfast - well done Marriott. The beach was ok, a bit rocky, but the kids enjoyed it. The only serious complaint I have was the slow service at all the dining venues. We stayed for 4 days and took to ordering food at least 1 hour in advance because it took so long to come and the children were crying with hunger the first lunch time. On our last day we had to leave by 1pm and went into the small Java cafe to grab a sandwich to eat in the car on the way. We ordered at 12.15pm (very simple sandwiches, a coffee and an iced chocolate) and by 12.45pm we had received nothing - not even the coffee. There was only one other group of 3 people in the cafe and I noticed that they hadn't received anything either! After enquiring at least twice, we were told at 12.45pm that it would be "another 10 minutes". I told the lady I could make 3 sandwiches in 5 minutes so why couldn't the 3 chefs I could see through the window? Of course, she had no answer, so we walked out. Not only did the Marriott miss out on US$30 worth of F&B, but we left with a sour experience. Very unfortunate as the hotel itself and the reception staff etc were fantastic.
reviews.positives: Rooms were beautiful and comfortably large, pools were fantastic for the kids, breakfast was excellent

reviews.title: Ok, But needs some work
reviews.text:
This is a new resort, about 1 year old. It's an odd place though. Really new and modern. The ocean view is nice, but they put a huge grass field and an odd 'garden' in the middle of the grounds. Just looks out of place. The lobby is really modern, but it's open, like the wind wipps through and it's hot and sticky as Thailand is 356 days a year. I prefer the blast of cool air when I walk into a lobby. One big plus here is that it has a ton of things for families with small children. 

It has positives and negatives. Positive - Really modern, both in features and design. Negative - the modern features in the room make it difficult to figure the room out. Can't find the high-tech light switches when you get up at night to go to the bathroom, the automatic closet light doesn't go out when you close the closet and you can see it at night. The windows are huge, modern windows, but they have so many curtains, they are falling off the hooks and the darkening curtains hang open allowing the early 6 am light to come through the window and wake you up. The restaurant has huge modern glass doors, two of them don't work and the workers leave them stuck open and the air conditioning pours outside. 

Nice in-room resort brochures and other hotel information, but when you ask about a activity or promotion, they either don't know anything about it, or "they don't have it today." Nice security features on the door, but in the evening while we were out, the maid (I hope) came in our room and closed the curtains without permission. Huge buffet, but the food was less than impressive and a bit dry (compared to hotels in this class like the Dusit Hua Hin). I would get a new chef. 

The hotel is large and impressive, but there is NOTHING, I mean nothing, not even a mini-mart or 7-11 for miles. 20 minutes driving your car to the nearest 7-11 and 40 minutes to the nearest civilization. There isn't a restaurant or anything on the road for miles.

reviews.title: Deserted
reviews.text:
The hotel is nice, rooms very pleasant, clean.
Amazing swimming pool, you can swim next to sharks, which are in an aquarium adjacent to the pool. The pool wines it’s way thru the hotel complex, with beach areas, slides, and ends up with an infinity pool over looking the beach.
The is a lack of maintenance to the complex, with loose tiles, Brocken steps and drain covers. The sort of thing which is normal in Thailand, but I did not expect in a Marriot Hotel.
The hotel is remote, with only one local bar in walking distance a short way up the beach. We eat and drank in this bar all the time, so I can not comment on the hotel food.
Lovely beach, water not crystal clear, but OK
The was no hotel bar that I could find, just a terrace that you could order beer.
Everything closed early, due to lack of guests, I assume this is due to covid and people only just starting to return.
If you want a holiday we’re you spend all your time in a hotel complex, with the odd organised day trip, this hotel is ideal. If you like roaming about, eating in local restaurants, and exploring markets, forget it.

reviews.title: Mixed Stay
reviews.text: While the hotel is quite good, there were quite some minor issues. Check-in took unbearably long and they claimed they hadn't read my message that I had added while booking, asking for twin beds in one room and double in the other. The reply was that the floor didn't have twin beds, but I didn't choose the floor myself. Eventually it was solved by 4 staff (!!!), and they did supply an extra bed free of charge.  On another day one staff certainly wasn't having a good day taking my order and let it show from the first moment they approached. At breakfast the 5 drink containers were all empty on two days in a row at around 09:30 and it took quite a while to fill, and only water was filled one day, and on the other day the water was filled leaving all the juice containers empty. Later at the bar even though we confirmed it was happy hour, the drinks were charged at full price and we had to ask to have the bill changed. Other than that, happy I could bring my cat, and the pools were quite good.

The room looked worn out, and the view wasn't panoramic as advertised; the Standard room actually had a better view. reviews.title: 35th Wedding Anniversary Celebrations
reviews.text: We recently celebrated our 35th Wedding Anniversary at Rayong Marriott Resort and Spa. Upon booking our accommodations on line, I mentioned re-our wedding anniversary and left it at that not expecting anything. We certainly did not anticipate or expect to be greeting with Anniversary congratulations; balloons; a beautiful arrangement of flowers and towels on the bed, an artistic drawing with words of congratulations and a small cake.  We were so touched with the intentionality that went into arranging this very special surprise. It was a very pleasant way to begin what turned out to be a very relaxing and enjoyable escape from the hustle and bustle of Bangkok!  We are so thankful to the staff for paying special attention to the few remarks  made upon registration.  We did not request or expect anything at all! A special word of thanks to the 2 ladies from house keeping who did an amazing job of decorating our room!
reviews.response:
Dear Glen, 
What a joy it is to read your review! We are absolutely thrilled that we could be a part of such a significant milestone, your 35th Wedding Anniversary, and that our small gestures brought so much delight to your celebration. We truly believe in creating memorable moments for our guests.

It would be our distinct pleasure to welcome you back again for many more joyous occasions.

Best Regards,
Rayong Marriott Resort & Spa

reviews.text:
Overall, we liked it. But it was not as we expected it. It was not super clean as we would expect from Marriott. Also, they seemed to be short on staff in the main restaurant "Salt". Breakfast was a bit chaotic. We had to wait to get the table, there were not enough mugs for coffee/tea. Some food was finished and not replaced. We had to wait for quite a bit to get an omlet. We sat outside on the balcony a few times right at the entrance of the sliding doors. There was a coffee station outside and right next to it, there they collected dirty dishes. You could see staff scraping the plates and there was a certain smell too. It killed my appetite. 
When we came to order dinner at the same restaurant, they told us that there was only one chef and to get a pizza we needed to wait one hour.. and that’s how long it took. 
The pools were not super clean either. The water was greenish. When my kids dove with the mask on, they could see a lot of things floating around. 
We liked the lobby area to sit and chill. The seafood restaurant was good and the seefood was delicious.

reviews.title: Nice, relaxing weekend
reviews.text:
I arrived on my motorbike so I parked in the car park on the ground floor. The lobby is on the second floor so, not knowing where the lifts were, I had to walk up the stairs to get to the lobby. The check-in was pleasant though the girls there seemed a little giggly to themselves. 

The deluxe mountain view room was really nice. The beds are really comfortable! There's a rain type shower where the water pours from the ceiling and is pretty powerful and hot. The drainage in the shower isn't very good though, not because of the plumbing but because the floor seemed to not let the water flow into the drain well enough. Overall, the bedrooms are really nice and comfortable with very little complaints.

There are two swimming pools: one smaller with its own little beach directly connected to it, and a much larger one which appears to flow "infinitely" into the sea. The views here especially during the sunset are stunning. The beach is nice and clean and the sea is clean too.

The cafe where the breakfast is served is nice and spacious and the breakfast is nice and has quite a lot of variety. I didn't try any other meals in the hotel so I can't comment on lunch/dinner.

The hotel is quite far from anything else, with the nearest 7/11 and local restaurants being about a 10 minute drive away. There is a lovely Thai restaurant called Jo Jo which serves the most delicious Thai food I've had and is pretty cheap too. 

The check-out was OK though I didn't feel like the staff made much of an effort to make me feel like returning or asked if my stay was nice or anything.

Overall, a really lovely stay and would recommend to anyone who wants to get away from the busy life for a few days. Also, if you're interested in visiting Koh Samet for a day, the hotel has a shuttle bus for free that leaves the hotel at 10am and picks you up from the pier at 4:30pm.
reviews.positives: Really comfortable bed, beautiful sunset/view from the pool, easy to get to Koh Samet

Drove down for a couple nights. The location is a bit remote an without a car, it would be very limiting. That said, it was quiet and serene which was the point. The beach was fun for looking at shells and jellyfish but certainly not for swimming. Pools were warm and comfortable. The room was not large but had a lovely ocean view. Some minor repairs are needed as things like door handles are coming loose. Beds were comfortable and the bathroom (after closing sliding doors) was more than adequate. The staff were excellent all around and breakfast was very, very good. A huge amount of choices and very good quality. 
Overall a very nice stay.

reviews.title: 4 star hotel
reviews.text:
We only stayed one night at the Marriott and that was enough for us, we had two rooms on the 5th floor which were excellent, the problem was getting to them, had to wait a very long time for the lifts (very slow). The hotel has a great location looking out over the sea, but the amount of people staying there spoils it as it is crowded which creates a lot of noise so is not very relaxing.
We had lunch down by the sea at the Fish Bar, the food was good but the sun blinds do not really work as the sun can still shine through them so there are limited numbers of tables in the shade, the rest a in the full sun. We asked for our two separate bills so we could pay for our food, I paid in cash and when my friend paid by credit card, the waiter placed his finger on the bill and pointed to the word "Tip"! We pointed out that there was a 10% service charge already included and we will not be paying an extra "Tip". When I got my change back from my bill they had left out the 95 satang, I pointed this out and they told me that they round the bill up! Now 95 satang is nothing to me but I felt that there was a scam going on so I asked at reception what the policy was and was told they should give the full change back. So if they do this with every foreign tourist then they will be making a bit extra and stealing at the same time.
Would I go back to this hotel? In the busy holiday season NO, but in the low season with out to many tourist Yes.

reviews.title: Nice location but improvement needed
reviews.text:
This hotel is located at a nice beach with rocky and also a large sandy stretch. Water is very shallow so it is very suitable for small kids as well. I think this is the main target group of the hotel as there were plenty of families with small kids. The pool area was quite crowded in the afternoons but quiet during lunchtime. rooms are large and well furnished but there were ptoblems with power supply. Waiting lines at the elevator were quite long as only 3 out of 4 elevators were working. Expect long lines for check in and check out as the staff takes ages to handle 1 party, especially on weekends. Anyway the management seems to need real improvement to make this hotel a 5 Star hotel again. maybe it is caused by Covid impact? Breakfast is fantastic and so was the service. Staff is really friendly and very helpful - 5 Stars for them!
Balcony on Deluxe Rooms is really small but has nice views - if you enjoy the hot weather.

reviews.text: Nice room and ocean view.  Good breakfast buffet line, varieties of food.  Very large swimming pool.  A bit of a walk to the beach.

reviews.title: Outstanding
reviews.positives: This was our favourite stay in Thailand and we have been more than 15 times! This time we travelled with our 2 year old and this hotel is perfect if you are looking for a place to relax. The design is amazing and the rooms are huge with super comfy bed, sofa and bath. The resort is really well looked after and super clean. There are 3 pools, slides, swings, kids club, everything you need. The beach it is in is quiet and you can easily walk to other placed to eat. Getting there was really easy from Bangkok. The staff were all so so friendly and the breakfast is so yummy.
